what_it_does: A noticeboard note that records a process observation: once a new agent landed, prior references saying the system was waiting for that agent should have been updated, and the practice of doing so should be documented.
observations: Posted note by tim in the dragnet-development channel
Comments on board://item-79686491 via a commented_on link
Body contains a [section] marker followed by a short maintenance/process observation
Refers to 'previous stuff that talked about waiting for that fifth Agent' without naming specific files
Suggests a discipline of updating stale 'waiting' references and recording that discipline somewhere
Has standard noticeboard frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history
standouts: The core signal is the observation that stale 'waiting for the fifth Agent' references should be updated when the agent actually lands
It explicitly calls out that leaving old 'waiting' language in place is unhelpful
It proposes that the discipline for handling this should be recorded somewhere
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, timestamps, and history
Uses a markdown body with a [section] tag to delimit the note content
Uses a commented_on link to relate the note to another board item
concerns: The referenced 'previous stuff' and 'fifth Agent' are unspecified, so the note is not actionable without external context

notes: A process note flagging that stale 'waiting for agent' references need cleanup once the agent lands, and that the cleanup discipline should be documented.
questions: Which specific prior content or files referred to waiting for the fifth Agent?

purpose_vs_actual: The title 'Tim · section' is only a label; the actual content is a maintenance/process observation, so the stated purpose is implicit rather than explicit.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish or advocate for a documented practice: when an agent lands, retroactively update any references that said the team was waiting for it, so the ledger remains accurate and useful.
novelty: It treats an agent becoming available as a lifecycle event that triggers retroactive documentation updates, rather than only forward-looking changes.
contribution: Captures a concrete process lesson about cleaning up stale 'waiting' references after an agent lands, which a fused system could keep as a maintenance/onboarding reminder.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by tim in dragnet-development observing that prior references to waiting for a fifth Agent should be updated once the agent lands and suggesting the cleanup discipline be recorded.
intention_for_embedding: To record a process observation about maintaining accurate references after an agent is onboarded.
